diagrams.title: Chapman-Kolmogorov Equation Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows a 3-state Markov chain (states A, B, C) with arrows representing transitions. Demonstrates how to compute the 2-step transition probability P²(i,j) by summing over all possible paths through intermediate state k: P²(i,j) = Σₖ P(i,k) × P(k,j). The diagram shows direct paths and paths through intermediate states.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how the Chapman-Kolmogorov equation computes multi-step transition probabilities by considering all possible intermediate states, a fundamental concept for understanding n-step transitions in Markov chains

diagrams.title: State Classification in Markov Chains
diagrams.description: Displays a Markov chain with different state types clearly labeled: transient state (state 1 - can exit and never return), recurrent/absorbing state (state 4 - self-loop with probability 1), periodic state (state 2 - forms a cycle), and aperiodic state (state 3 - self-loop allows return at any time). Color-coded legend distinguishes each type.
elements: Classification legend (Transient, Recurrent, Absorbing, Periodic, Aperiodic)
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the classification of states in Markov chains - distinguishing transient vs recurrent, periodic vs aperiodic, and identifying absorbing states - which is essential for analyzing long-term behavior and convergence properties

diagrams.title: Transition Matrix Path Decomposition
diagrams.description: Shows a 2×2 transition probability matrix and visualizes the matrix multiplication concept of Chapman-Kolmogorov. Displays how (P²)ᵢⱼ = Σₖ PᵢₖPₖⱼ represents the sum of probabilities of all length-2 paths from state i to state j, with each path shown as a branching tree diagram.
elements: Branching path tree from state i
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the matrix formulation of Chapman-Kolmogorov equations, showing how matrix multiplication inherently implements the equation and how to decompose n-step transitions into sum of paths through intermediate states
